mission.title: Install and Configure Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE)
mission.description: This mission guides you through the installation of the Vultr Kubernetes Engine (VKE), a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Vultr's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a straightforward way to leverage Kubernetes without managing the underlying infrastructure, but it may incur costs based on resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Vultr CLI
steps.description:
The Vultr CLI is required to interact with the Vultr API for creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/vultr/vultr-cli/releases/download/v1.0.0/vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf vultr-cli_linux_amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv vultr-cli /usr/local/bin/

vultr-cli version

```

> Download the latest Vultr CLI
> Extract the downloaded tarball
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH
> Verify the installation

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Vultr with the specified version and node count.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s create --region 'ewr' --version '1.30' --node-count 3 --plan 'vc2-2c-4gb'

```

> Replace 'ewr' with your desired region

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s kubeconfig --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> > ~/.kube/config

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster to ensure optimal performance.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/vultr/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/monitoring/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy the cluster autoscaler
> Deploy monitoring tools

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

vultr-cli k8s delete --cluster-id <your-cluster-id>

```

> Replace <your-cluster-id> with the actual cluster ID

uninstall.title: Remove Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure to manually check and remove any orphaned resources such as load balancers and persistent volumes.

```bash

vultr-cli loadbalancer list

```

> List load balancers to check for orphaned resources

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your data and verify the current state of your cluster.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces > backup-$(date +%F).yaml

vultr-cli k8s upgrade --cluster-id <your-cluster-id> --version '1.31'

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current resources
> Upgrade to the latest version
> Verify nodes are in 'Ready' status after upgrade

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the kubeconfig setup command with the correct cluster ID.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Node status is NotReady
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Nodes may not have sufficient resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check node resource allocation and ensure they meet the minimum requirements.

troubleshooting.title: Error: LoadBalancer service not created
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Vultr Cloud Controller Manager may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the CCM is deployed and configured with the correct API key and region.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Failed to scale the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Cluster autoscaler may not be configured properly.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster autoscaler configuration and ensure it has the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Vultr regions and improved load balancer integration.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
